versionNotes: Sprint 4 W13 Gap Statement — no-AI form activity, 5 required fields + rejection criteria confirmation
title: Gap Statement
description: Based on your retrospective, name the ONE gap you'll close in Sprint 4. This is the most important thing you'll write all sprint.

questions.infoBlock:
Field 1 of 5 · The gap

One sentence. Name the SDL dimension AND the specific sub-skill you haven't developed. The dimension keeps you anchored in learning. The sub-skill keeps you honest about what specifically you haven't done.
questions.prompt: One sentence naming the SDL dimension and the specific sub-skill you haven't developed.
questions.placeholder: Example: Metacognition — I can't notice when I've stopped learning and started performing.

questions.infoBlock:
Field 2 of 5 · The evidence this gap is real

Two or three references to specific artifacts from your retrospective that show this gap — not just claim it. Where did you rate yourself "Weak evidence," "No evidence," or "Didn't engage" that points at this gap?
questions.prompt: Two or three specific references from your retrospective (assignments, moments, ratings) that show this gap is real.
questions.placeholder:
Example:
1. In my retrospective I rated Metacognition as "No evidence" — I couldn't name a single Sprint 1-3 moment where I caught myself mid-shortcut.
2. My Sprint 2 Week 7 check-in: I wrote "everything is fine" when I'd actually skipped two group meetings. I didn't notice the gap between what I wrote and what I was doing.
3. My Sprint 3 reflection on the futures brief — I defended a claim I hadn't actually engaged with because I thought the instructor wanted me to have one.

questions.infoBlock:
Field 3 of 5 · Why this gap and not another

One sentence. What makes this the one worth two weeks of your work?
questions.prompt: One sentence. What makes this the one worth two weeks of your work?
questions.placeholder: Example: If I can't catch myself performing instead of learning, I'll keep doing it in my first job and never know why I'm stuck.

questions.infoBlock:
Field 4 of 5 · The vehicle

What concrete activity, project, or practice will you use as the container for closing this gap? A technical skill (like learning a tool) can be the vehicle, but the gap itself has to be on a learning dimension, not the technical skill.
questions.prompt: What concrete activity, project, or practice will you use as the container for closing this gap?
questions.placeholder: Example: I'll use learning Next.js as the vehicle — but the work I'll document isn't "I shipped a Next.js app." It's a daily two-sentence journal where I catch myself either performing (copy-pasting from docs without understanding) or actually learning (writing something I could explain). The Next.js work is the excuse to generate those moments; the journal is the evidence of the gap closing.

questions.infoBlock:
Field 5 of 5 · What "closed" looks like

One sentence naming what observable evidence you'd need to show the gap is smaller in two weeks. NOT "I'll feel better at it." Something you could point at.
questions.prompt: One sentence naming observable evidence that the gap is smaller in two weeks. Not a feeling — something you could point at.
questions.placeholder: Example: At least five journal entries where I caught myself performing in real-time and changed what I was doing in the next 10 minutes — with the before/after visible in the same entry.

questions.infoBlock:
⚠️ What gets rejected

Before you export, check your Gap Statement against these four rejection criteria. If you see yourself in any of them, go back and rewrite.

1. Anchored in a technical skill, not a learning dimension

✗ "Learn React" — rejected.
✓ "Use React as a vehicle to practice ambiguity tolerance" — possible.

2. Too big to close in two weeks

✗ "Get better at planning" — rejected.
✓ "Develop the habit of writing a one-sentence plan before starting any task" — possible.

3. Too vague or abstract

✗ "Be more reflective" — rejected.

4. No evidence basis

✗ "I think I'm bad at collaboration" with nothing from your retrospective to back it up — rejected.
questions.prompt: Type the exact sentence: "I've read the rejection criteria above and my Gap Statement does not fall into any of them."
questions.placeholder: I've read the rejection criteria above and my Gap Statement does not fall into any of them.
